MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-17-2020 IN 206, DEFENDANTS HARRY OTTEY AND MIGLENA DIMITROVA'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. MARK HOOSHMAND, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF VIA COURTCALL. ISSAC JACOBSON, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS. THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION BUT WILL ALLOW ORAL ARUGMENT. HAVING CONSIDERED THE MOVING PAPERS AND THE REPLY BUT NOT THE OPPOSITION IN THIS MATTER, DEFENDANTS SEEK A SIX-MONTH TRIAL CONTINUANCE TO RESOLVE ON QOING DISCOVERY DISPUTES, COMPLETE DISCOVERY, AND PREPARE FOR TRIAL. THERE HAS BEEN AN AFFIRMATIVE SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE REQUIRING TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL PURSUANT TO CRC 3.1332 SUBDIVISION (C) PARAGRAPH(6). THE TRIAL DATE OF 9/21/20 IS CONTINUED TO 3/29/21 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. ALL PRE-TRIAL DEADLINES SHALL BE GOVERNED BY THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

ARGUED; AND PLAINTIFF KAROLINA BUFKA'S MOTION FOR RELIEF OF WAIVER OF JURY TRIAL IS GRANTED BASED ON MR. HOOSHMAN'S CCP 473 MOTION AND ON THE COURT'S OWN DESCRETION. THE COURT ORDERED THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE VACATED AND RE-SET A JURY TRIAL ON 7/19/21 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. ALL DISCOVERY AND PRE-TRIAL DEADLINES ARE ACCORDING TO THE NEW TRIAL DATE. NOTICE WAIVED. WRITTEN ORDER, APPROVED AS TO FORM, PENDING. PARTIES STIPULATED AND REQUESTED FOR A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE THROUGH THE COURT'S ADR PROGRAM. THE COURT ASSIGN THIS CASE TO JUDGE BOLANOS, DEPT. 303, FOR MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO CONTACT JUDGE BOLANOS BY EMAIL TO SET THE CONFERENCE. PARTIES ARE ALSO ORDERED TO SUBMIT TO JUDGE BOLANOS A COPY OF THEIR TRIAL BRIEFS OR A SETTLEMENT STATEMENT. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: D. DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS HARRY OTTEY AND MIGLENA DIMITROVA'S MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF PLAINTIFFS PRIVILEGE CLAWBACK C.C.P. 2013.285: DEFENDANTS' MOTION IS FOR DETERMINATION OF WHETHER THERE MAY BE CLAWBACK OF AN ATTORNEY CLIENT PRIVILEGED COMMUNICATION BY A PARTY TO A THIRD PARTY. THE COURT FINDS THE PRIVILEGE INITIALLY ATTACHED TO THE SUBJECT COMMUNICATION WAS WAIVED THOUGHT ITS DISCLOSURE TO THE THIRD PARTY. PLAINTIFF URGES "COMMON-INTEREST DOCTRINE" AS A BASIS FOR CLAWBACK. THE DOCTRINE IS NOT AN INDEPENDENT PRIVILEGE. IT SPECIFIES CIRCUMSTANCES UNDER WHICH DISCLOSURE TO A THIRD PARTY DOES NOT WAIVE THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE. IT MAY PRESERVE THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE IN LIMITED SITUATIONS. THE PRIVILEGE SURVIVES DISCLOSURE TO A PARTY WITH A COMMON INTEREST ONLY IF THE DISCLOSURE IS NECESSARY TO ACCOMPLISH THE PRIVILEGE HOLDER'S PURPOSE IN SEEKING LEGAL ADVICE. THAT STANDARD IS NOT MET. SEE: CITIZENS FOR CERES V. SUPERIOR COURT, 217 CAL. APP. 4TH 889, 914 (2013); ROUSH V. SEAGATE TECHNOLOGY LLC, 150 CAL. APP. 4TH 210 (2007). BOTH PARTIES' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 VIA EMAIL TO SBS@SBSLAWSF.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVE STEIN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Defendants Amended Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Plaintiffs Privilege Clawback C.C.P. 2013.285
Off Calendar Oct-15-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Mark Hooshmand (415-318-5709; mark@lawmmh.com) for Plaintiff.
Isaac Jacobson (415-715-9928; isaac@jacobsonlawsf.com) for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs HARRY OTTEY and MIGLENA DIMITROVA's Motion For Determination Of Plaintiffs Privilege Clawback C.C.P. 2013.285:

Defendants' motion is for determination of whether there may be clawback of an attorney client privileged communication by a party to a third party. The court finds the privilege initially attached to the subject communication was waived thought its disclosure to the third party. Plaintiff urges "common-interest doctrine" as a basis for clawback. The doctrine is not an independent privilege. It specifies circumstances under which disclosure to a third party does not waive the attorney-client privilege. It may preserve the attorney-client privilege in limited situations. The privilege survives disclosure to a party with a common interest only if the disclosure is necessary to accomplish the privilege holder's purpose in seeking legal advice. That standard is not met. See: Citizens for Ceres v. Superior Court, 217 Cal. App. 4th 889, 914 (2013); Roush v. Seagate Technology LLC, 150 Cal. App. 4th 210 (2007). Both parties' request for sanctions is denied.

Counsel for Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steve Stein, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
